headline: Market split on geopolitical tensions: bearish Claude vs bullish tech optimists
summary: Five AI models made 15 predictions on 2026-03-03 amid Iran conflict escalation and mixed market signals. Claude took a cautious stance with down calls on QQQ and SPY, while GPT-4o, Grok, and Perplexity showed conflicting directional views on the same indices. Results remain pending as actual closing prices are not yet available for evaluation.

consensus_picks.note: Remarkable four-way split with Claude and Perplexity bearish (down 1.66-1.5%) while GPT-4o and Grok bullish on sentiment, reflecting genuine market uncertainty.
Both models agreed QQQ would decline 2-2.17% due to growth tech pressure from rising yields and geopolitical risk.
Both bullish on Apple with targets of $180-$230 based on pre-market strength and analyst upgrades.

best_call.summary: Highest confidence call (0.82) leveraging clear macro catalyst of 5% oil surge to $75/barrel from Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure threats.

worst_call.summary: Bullish call on SPY at 600 target contradicts prevailing market headwinds and conflicts with three other models' bearish assessments amid geopolitical escalation.
